channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Several Republicans Break With Trump to Pass Ukraine Aid Bill
item.description: Eighteen Republican lawmakers joined Democrats on Thursday to pass a bill that would provide new aid to Ukraine, clashing with President Trump’s foreign policy agenda.
item.dc:creator: Julie Yoon

media:content.media:description: Ukrainian soldiers in the Donetsk region of eastern Ukraine in January. The House bill passed Thursday included $8 billion in loans to Ukraine and $1.8 billion in aid.

item.title: Pommelien Thijs, la estrella pop amada por la mitad de Bélgica
item.description: Las pegadizas canciones de Thijs son adoradas en Bélgica, al menos en la parte neerlandesa.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall and Ilvy Njiokiktjien

media:content.media:credit: Ilvy Njiokiktjien for The New York Times

item.title: Late Night Sizes Up Trump’s Reflecting-Pool Ambitions
item.description: “One thing we know for sure is that Trump is definitely obsessed with size,” Seth Meyers said of the president’s bragging about the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: El último techo de paja
item.description: Dentro de la minúscula comunidad de maestros techadores ingleses, se desarrolla una lucha por una tradición que podría no sobrevivir.
item.dc:creator: Rukmini Callimachi and Jeremie Souteyrat

item.title: Las granjas en EE. UU. dependen más que nunca de un problemático programa de visas
item.description: El gobierno de Trump está permitiendo la entrada de más trabajadores agrícolas invitados bajo el programa H-2A, pero evitar los abusos resulta difícil.
item.dc:creator: Lydia DePillis

media:content.media:credit: Grant Hindsley para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Christian Calderon es uno de los trabajadores con visa H-2A contratados para cosechar cerezas en Kennewick, Washington. Mientras el gobierno de Trump reduce los salarios, los agricultores solicitan más trabajadores extranjeros.

item.title: Qué significa ‘pulchritude’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en tres artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Why Harlem May Be the Deciding Factor in a Critical House Race
item.description: Representative Adriano Espaillat and his progressive challenger, Darializa Avila Chevalier, have zeroed in on Harlem, traditionally hostile turf for the Democratic incumbent.
item.dc:creator: Sally Goldenberg and Maya King

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Adriano Espaillat held a campaign rally with labor leaders in Harlem last week, part of an effort to court voters in an area where his support is weaker.

item.title: ‘Out of Control’: When Troopers and Protesters Clash, Questions Follow
item.description: Gov. Mikie Sherrill had hoped that replacing federal agents with the state police would bring peace. It didn’t work out that way.
item.dc:creator: Chelsia Rose Marcius and Mark Bonamo

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: As night fell outside the Delaney Hall immigration detention center in Newark, clashes between demonstrators and law enforcement officers sometimes ensued.

item.title: Driven From Lenapehoking, They Hope to Return to New York
item.description: The Lenape were forced out of their ancestral lands centuries ago. Now in Oklahoma, their descendants feel the pull of homeland in the east.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Maslin Nir
item.category: National Museum of the American Indian

media:content.media:credit: Elias Williams for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Curtis Zunigha, a former chief of the Delaware Tribe of Indians in Bartlesville, Okla., has been making regular trips back to Lenapehoking, “the peoples’ land.”

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for June 5, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: ‘Mexico 86’ Review: This Underdog Has Bags of Cash
item.description: Diego Luna plays an ambitious soccer enthusiast who is trying to secure the World Cup for Mexico a second time.
item.dc:creator: Ben Kenigsberg

item.title: 2026 Tony Awards Predictions: Who Will Win?
item.description: Expect wins for the musical “Schmigadoon!” and the play “Liberation,” and for the “Ragtime” stars Joshua Henry and Caissie Levy.
item.dc:creator: Michael Paulson
item.category: Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts
Two Strangers (Carry a Cake Across New York) (Play)

item.title: Word of the Day: pulchritude
item.description: This word has appeared in three articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: China Builds an Economic Fortress as Global Tensions Rise
item.description: Beijing says the changes are needed for national security, but they could complicate efforts by Chinese companies to find growth overseas.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao

media:content.media:description: A robot made by a Chinese company displayed in April at an expo in Guangzhou, China. Beijing unveiled rules this week that would slow the overseas expansion of Chinese companies.

item.title: Trump Wants Results Against Crime in Mexico. Can Sheinbaum Deliver?
item.description: Trump wants results against organized crime. Can Mexico’s president deliver?
item.dc:creator: Carlos Bravo Regidor

media:content.media:credit: Luis Antonio Rojas for The New York Times

item.title: The Priests Revered in the Land of Five Genders
item.description: The bissus of South Sulawesi are considered a link between the earthly and celestial because they are thought to embody both male and female traits.
item.dc:creator: Sui-Lee Wee, Hasya Nindita and Nyimas Laula

media:content.media:description: Every year, the bissu in the Indonesian village of Segeri lead a ritual before the rice planting season. This procession, last November, included a sacred plow.

item.description: A self-stabbing ritual known as the ma’giri.
While reciting sacred mantras, the bissus made offerings to honor their ancestors and ward off misfortune.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Ireland, Seen as a Weak Link in Europe’s Defense, Tries to Bulk Up
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Friday, June 5, 2026.

item.title: Russia Rails Against the West but Welcomes Candace Owens and Andrew Tate
item.description: While some powerful Russians shun the West, others want to restore ties and embrace friendly Westerners. President Vladimir V. Putin’s annual economic conference illustrates the conflicting impulses.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko

media:content.media:description: A screen with an image of President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia at the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum on Wednesday.

item.title: A Rainy Japanese Rock Festival Proved They Could Weather Anything
item.description: Ana Escalante and Robert Lucas didn’t especially like each other through most of high school, but a coffee date their senior year changed all that.
item.dc:creator: Donna Kim

item.title: Corrections: June 5, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Friday, June 5, 2026.

item.title: He’s Famous for His Mukbangs. But They Met the Old-Fashioned Way.
item.description: Tommy Winkler, a TikToker known as “The Food Guy,” threw pebbles at Jess Pappas’s window when they were college students with Covid.
item.dc:creator: Tammy LaGorce

item.title: In a Relationship Between Two Puppeteers, Both Pull the Strings
item.description: Marta MacRostie and Jonathan Levin ran in the same circles, but it took a solar eclipse to bring them together.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Menza

item.title: A Wedding With ‘a Housewife in Attendance’ (Sort of)
item.description: Scott Winston and Nicholas Hunt, who share a love for reality TV and karaoke, spent their whole first date laughing.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Felder

item.title: Quil Lemons, the Photographer, Marries Stylist Parker Harwood
item.description: After getting into a car accident shortly after meeting, the photographer Quil Lemons made one call: to ask Parker Harwood, a stylist, on a date.
item.dc:creator: Sadiba Hasan

item.title: I Couldn’t Tell Him Why We Had to Break Up
item.description: When my young cousin’s girlfriend cheated on him, I wrestled with ghosts of my own.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Stone

item.title: Raman Gains on Pratt in L.A. Mayor Race But Many Ballots Remain
item.description: Nithya Raman’s vote count still remains well behind Spencer Pratt’s, but hundreds of thousands of ballots remain to be counted in Los Angeles over the next few weeks.
item.dc:creator: William P. Davis

item.title: What To Know as China’s Xi Jinping Heads to North Korea
item.description: As Xi Jinping visits Pyongyang, he faces an emboldened North Korean dictator, whose alliance with Russia has reduced his dependence on China.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson and Choe Sang-Hun
item.category: Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas

media:content.media:description: Xi Jinping, China’s leader, with North Korea’s leader, Kim Jong-un, in Beijing in September. Analysts say China is likely keen to assert its influence over North Korea as Pyongyang has leaned toward Russia.

item.title: Platner Denies Hurting Ex-Girlfriend and Says He Will Not Quit Senate Race
item.description: In an interview after The Times reported on his treatment of women he had dated, Graham Platner acknowledged “not exactly acting with the best behavior” after his military service.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:description: Graham Platner, the Democratic candidate for Senate in Maine, said in an interview he would “absolutely take responsibility” for elements of his personal history.

item.title: Court Reverses Convictions of Paramedics in Elijah McClain’s Death
item.description: A Colorado appeals court ordered new trials for Peter Cichuniec and Jeremy Cooper, convicted in 2023 after injecting Mr. McClain with ketamine. The court cited errors in jury instructions.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

media:content.media:description: A Colorado court reversed homicide convictions against two paramedics in the death of Elijah McClain, who died in 2019 after he was given a ketamine injection.

item.title: Murder Charge Dropped for Arkansas Man Nominated for Lonoke County Sheriff
item.description: A judge dismissed the charge against Aaron Spencer, the Republican nominee for Lonoke County sheriff, citing missing evidence and misconduct by law enforcement officials.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:description: Aaron Spencer had been accused of killing his daughter’s abuser before the dismissal.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for June 05, 2026
item.description: Kelly Morenus opens our solving weekend.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Matthew Odom for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ready to take a swing at this Friday themeless?

item.title: Schlossberg, Bores, Conway and Lasher Spar in NYC Primary Debate
item.description: Four Democratic hopefuls seeking to represent a key Manhattan House district clashed in the first debate of the race.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes

item.title: ‘Girl, Interrupted’ Review: A Musical That’s a Little Too Tranquilized
item.description: While the 1999 movie went for melodrama, this stage adaptation with songs by Aimee Mann honors the memoir’s coolly clinical prose.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

media:content.media:description: The cast of “Girl, Interrupted” at the Public Theater in Manhattan, from left: Mia Pak, Sally Shaw, Juliana Canfield, Katherine Reis, Ta’Rea Campbell and King Princess.

item.title: ‘Office Romance’ Review: Prepare for Turbulence
item.description: Jennifer Lopez and Brett Goldstein cause professional headaches in this workplace comedy about an airline.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Kennedy

item.title: What to Know About California’s Top Election Races
item.description: The Republicans Steve Hilton, who is running for governor, and Spencer Pratt, running for Los Angeles mayor, have shown early strength, but that could fade as more ballots are counted.
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan and Livia Albeck-Ripka

media:content.media:credit: Ariana Drehsler for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Voters in Los Angeles on Tuesday.

item.title: Theater Reminds Us That We’re Human
item.description: Ahead of the 2026 Tony Awards, Wesley Morris invites Helen Shaw, The Times’s chief theater critic, to help him understand this season of Broadway plays.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Rocklin, Austin Mitchell and Felice Leon

item.title: Las cadenas hoteleras se retiran de Cuba ante las presiones de Trump
item.description: Los esfuerzos del gobierno de Trump para presionar a Cuba parecen estar funcionando, ya que más empresas internacionales han anunciado que abandonarán la isla.
item.dc:creator: Frances Robles

media:content.media:description: El Hotel Catedral de La Habana es uno de los 15 hoteles de Cuba gestionados por la empresa española Meliá.

item.title: House Passes Ukraine Aid in Defiance of Republican Leaders
item.description: Eighteen G.O.P. lawmakers broke with their party and joined Democrats to deliver yet another blow to the president’s foreign policy agenda.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison

media:content.media:description: Ukrainian soldiers in the Donetsk region of eastern Ukraine in January. The House bill passed Thursday included $8 billion in loans to Ukraine and $1.8 billion in aid.

item.title: How Trump’s Proposed Arch Could Complicate D.C.’s Congested Airspace
item.description: The mammoth arch would sit directly in the paths of flights in and out of Reagan National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil, Marco Hernandez, Junho Lee and Karoun Demirjian

item.title: In a First, Scientists Precisely Edit Human Embryo Genes
item.description: Researchers relied on a newer gene-editing technique that may make it possible to engineer embryos, a prospect that has long alarmed bioethicists.
item.dc:creator: Carl Zimmer

media:content.media:description: Researchers altered genes that influence levels of LDL cholesterol and hemoglobin in the blood.

item.title: Zelensky Mixes Taunts and Peace Talks Offer in Letter to Putin
item.description: “After 26 years in power, age is beginning to take its toll,” President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine wrote of his Russian counterpart, bragging of a recent strike on St. Petersburg.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer

media:content.media:description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine, speaking in Sweden last week.

item.title: Pilot Was Warned Jet Was Too Low Before It Clipped a Light Pole
item.description: The pilot heard a “thump” while landing in Newark, according to a newly released report. The light pole crashed onto a truck.
item.dc:creator: Patrick McGeehan

item.title: Read Nick Bilton’s Memo to ‘60 Minutes’ Staff
item.description: The new executive producer of the program sought to lift staff morale after recent high-profile turmoil.
item.dc:creator: By

item.title: Reto del verano: Sal al aire libre y ‘a que te dé el sol’
item.description: Tu celular puede distraerte, pero no te recarga las pilas. Esto es lo que sí lo hace.
item.dc:creator: Jancee Dunn

item.title: ‘Scary Movie’ Review: Too Many Movies, Too Few Laughs
item.description: The sixth installment of this low-grade franchise takes a predictable and dismaying turn, even if it feels like the right time to bring the series back.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Yu

item.title: Ohio State Agrees to $100 Million Payout Over Sexual Abuse Claims
item.description: The deal with 279 former students is the latest in the long-running case at the university. In all, nearly 600 people who said they were abused by an athletic department doctor have settled.
item.dc:creator: Billy Witz

media:content.media:description: A sexual abuse scandal has enveloped Ohio State University since 2018, when a former student came forward with claims that he was assaulted by an athletic team doctor.

item.title: New Research Suggests a Possible Path to Prevent Lung Cancer
item.description: Also, senators clashed over Trump’s payout fund. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

item.title: California Businessman Accused of Selling Restricted Technology to Iran
item.description: Jamshid Ghomi, of Newport Coast, Calif., was charged with conspiracy to violate the International Emergency Economic Powers Act.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker
item.category: Office of Foreign Assets Control (United States Treasury)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A woman walking near a billboard in Tehran last month. Federal prosecutors charged the chief executive of an Iranian-based technology company with conspiring to evade U.S. restrictions on technology.
